description: Add aws_db_proxy_secret and aws_db_proxy_policy_attachment resources to manage RDS Proxy secrets and IAM policies independently from the proxy resource

userStories.title: Create aws_db_proxy_secret resource skeleton and schema
userStories.description: As a contributor, I need the resource file scaffolding with schema so that CRUD operations can be implemented.
acceptanceCriteria: File internal/service/rds/proxy_secret.go exists with @SDKResource or @FrameworkResource annotation
Resource schema defines db_proxy_name (Required, ForceNew, string), secret_arn (Required, ForceNew, string), and id (Computed)
CRUD function stubs exist (create, read, delete) even if not yet implemented
Resource is registered in the provider via make gen
Code compiles (go build ./...)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow existing proxy_*.go file patterns in internal/service/rds/. Use findDBProxyByName() and waiter patterns from proxy.go.

userStories.title: Implement Create and Delete for aws_db_proxy_secret
userStories.description: As a Terraform user, I want to create and delete a proxy-secret association so that my RDS Proxy can authenticate using credentials stored in Secrets Manager.
acceptanceCriteria: Create calls ModifyDBProxy to add the secret ARN to the proxy's Auth configuration
Delete calls ModifyDBProxy to remove the secret ARN from the proxy's auth configuration
Handles the proxy's modifying/available status with appropriate waiters
Error handling follows provider conventions (create.AppendDiagError, resource-not-found detection)
Code compiles and make lint passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reuse findDBProxyByName(), waitDBProxyUpdated(), expandUserAuthConfigs(), flattenUserAuthConfigInfos() from proxy.go.

userStories.title: Implement Read and Import for aws_db_proxy_secret
userStories.description: As a Terraform user, I want to read and import a proxy-secret association so I can manage existing associations.
acceptanceCriteria: Read calls DescribeDBProxies and checks that the secret ARN is present in the proxy's auth list
Read removes from state if the secret ARN is not found (handles out-of-band deletion)
Import ID format is db_proxy_name/secret_arn
terraform import aws_db_proxy_secret.example my-proxy/arn:aws:secretsmanager:... populates state correctly
Code compiles and make lint passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create aws_db_proxy_policy_attachment resource skeleton and schema
userStories.description: As a contributor, I need the resource file scaffolding for the policy attachment resource.
acceptanceCriteria: File internal/service/rds/proxy_policy_attachment.go exists with resource registration annotation
Resource schema defines db_proxy_name (Required, ForceNew, string), policy_name (Required, ForceNew, string), policy_document (Required, string, JSON IAM policy), and id (Computed)
CRUD function stubs exist (create, read, update, delete)
Resource is registered in the provider via make gen
Code compiles (go build ./...)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This resource operates on IAM, not RDS directly. It looks up the proxy's RoleArn via DescribeDBProxies then attaches policy to that role.

userStories.title: Implement Create and Delete for aws_db_proxy_policy_attachment
userStories.description: As a Terraform user, I want to attach and remove an IAM policy on an RDS Proxy's execution role so the proxy can access secrets.
acceptanceCriteria: Create looks up the proxy's IAM RoleArn via DescribeDBProxies
Create attaches the policy to the role (inline via PutRolePolicy or managed via CreatePolicy+AttachRolePolicy)
Delete removes the policy from the execution role
Error handling follows provider conventions
Code compiles and make lint passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Determine inline vs managed approach based on existing provider patterns (aws_iam_role_policy uses inline, aws_iam_role_policy_attachment uses managed).

userStories.title: Implement Read, Update, and Import for aws_db_proxy_policy_attachment
userStories.description: As a Terraform user, I want to read, update, and import a policy attachment.
acceptanceCriteria: Read verifies the policy is still attached to the proxy's execution role; removes from state if not found
Update supports updating policy_document in-place
Import ID format is db_proxy_name/policy_name
terraform import aws_db_proxy_policy_attachment.example my-proxy/rds-proxy-access populates state correctly
Code compiles and make lint passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Acceptance tests for aws_db_proxy_secret
userStories.description: As a contributor, I need comprehensive acceptance tests to validate the proxy secret resource.
acceptanceCriteria: TestAccRDSProxySecret_basic test creates a proxy-secret association, verifies it exists, destroys it
TestAccRDSProxySecret_disappears test verifies graceful handling when the association is removed outside Terraform
Import state verification step included in basic test
testAccCheckRDSProxySecretExists() helper implemented
testAccCheckRDSProxySecretDestroy() helper implemented
Base test config reuses common infrastructure via acctest.ConfigCompose()
Tests use sdkacctest.RandomWithPrefix(acctest.ResourcePrefix) for resource naming
make lint passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow patterns from proxy_test.go. Tests must pass in us-west-2. Use resource.ParallelTest().

userStories.title: Acceptance tests for aws_db_proxy_policy_attachment
userStories.description: As a contributor, I need comprehensive acceptance tests to validate the policy attachment resource.
acceptanceCriteria: TestAccRDSProxyPolicyAttachment_basic test creates a policy attachment, verifies it exists, destroys it
TestAccRDSProxyPolicyAttachment_disappears test verifies graceful handling when the policy is removed outside Terraform
TestAccRDSProxyPolicyAttachment_update test verifies updating policy_document works correctly
Import state verification step included in basic test
testAccCheckRDSProxyPolicyAttachmentExists() helper implemented
testAccCheckRDSProxyPolicyAttachmentDestroy() helper implemented
make lint passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow patterns from proxy_test.go. Tests must pass in us-west-2. Use resource.ParallelTest().

userStories.title: Documentation for aws_db_proxy_secret
userStories.description: As a Terraform user, I want reference documentation so I know how to use the proxy secret resource.
acceptanceCriteria: File website/docs/r/db_proxy_secret.html.markdown exists
Includes page title, subcategory, description, example usage, argument reference, attribute reference, import section
Example shows creating a Secrets Manager secret and associating it with a proxy
Import section documents the db_proxy_name/secret_arn format
make docs-lint passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the format of website/docs/r/db_proxy.html.markdown and other existing proxy resource docs.

userStories.title: Documentation for aws_db_proxy_policy_attachment
userStories.description: As a Terraform user, I want reference documentation for the policy attachment resource.
acceptanceCriteria: File website/docs/r/db_proxy_policy_attachment.html.markdown exists
Includes page title, subcategory, description, example usage, argument reference, attribute reference, import section
Example shows creating an IAM policy document and attaching it to a proxy
Import section documents the db_proxy_name/policy_name format
make docs-lint passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the format of existing proxy resource docs.

userStories.title: Changelog entry for new resources
userStories.description: As a contributor, I need to add a changelog entry for the new resources.
acceptanceCriteria: A .changelog file is created with entries for both new resources
Follows existing changelog format (e.g., aws_db_proxy_secret: New resource)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PR number may not be known yet; use a placeholder if needed.
